_meta.name: GoodMedika
_meta.description: Desktop-native port of ghost_shell_browser/scripts/smart-search-and-click.json. Uses the new desktop-runtime handlers for `commercial_inflate` and `search_query` (Phase 38) so the legacy web logic executes 1:1 without macro emulation. Structural translations vs. the web original: `loop`→`foreach`, `item_var`→`params.var`, top-level `steps`→`body`, `then_steps`→`then`, `else_steps`→`else`, single-brace `{item}`→double-brace `{{item}}`. Comma-, newline-, and CR-delimited `items` strings all split correctly (Phase 38 fix).

name: GoodMedika
description: Brand SERP rotation with commercial pre-warm. Click any non-own ad (competitors + targets). For target-only: set only_on_target=true on click_ad — the outer ad_is_external is permissive enough to let it filter inside. For pure competitors: switch the outer condition kind to ad_is_competitor.

steps._comment: Top-level loop over brand keywords. The desktop foreach honors newline-delimited items in the same string so the existing 'one keyword per line' UX from the web carries over. shuffle=true randomises iteration order so we don't always hit Google with the same brand sequence.

body._comment: Pre-warm Google's commercial-intent context. probability=0.3 means ~30% of iterations get the warm-up; the rest fire the brand search cold (variation = more natural). click_organic=true gives a stronger signal at +~10s per pre-query. Native handler is now in ScriptRunner — no macro emulation.

Brand search. max_attempts=4 means search_query refreshes the SERP up to 4 times if the first response has no ads (handles soft-blocks). retry_min/max gives the same 13-15s gap the legacy web used. fail_on_empty=false means an all-empty result set still lets the script continue to the next iteration's brand keyword instead of aborting.

Walk every ad on the SERP. shuffle=true so we don't always click the top slot first (more natural). The if(ad_is_external) gate filters to non-own ads (covers BOTH competitors AND target-domain ads). To click ONLY pure competitors, switch this condition's kind to ad_is_competitor.

ad_is_external = 'any ad NOT on my domain' (covers competitors AND target-domain ads). Lets the inner click_ad filter further via only_on_target / skip_on_target. To click ONLY pure competitors (excluding targets), change kind to ad_is_competitor.

then._comment: skip_on_my_domain stays as a paranoid safety net (the outer ad_is_external already excludes own domain, but if my_domains is misconfigured at runtime this is the second line of defence). only_on_target=false → click both competitors and targets. Flip to true if you want target-only. skip_on_blocked=true so the runner never opens domains in the global block list. Per-step ad-domain flags are top-level on the desktop step shape (not inside params).
Short between-click pause inside foreach_ad. probability=0.6 means most of the time we pause — but not always (foreach_ad already has its own scan-pause between iterations).

body._comment: Cool-down between query-loop iterations. Real users don't fire searches back-to-back without breathing room.
